title: Helping at the Café
summary: A boy helps his dad open the small family café in Rome on a busy Saturday morning.

body.speaker: Marco
body.text: Papa, what can I do first?

body.speaker: Papa
body.text: Good morning, Marco. Put the chairs on the floor, please. The cleaner left them on the tables.

body.speaker: Marco
body.text: Okay. Where does this small chair go?

body.speaker: Papa
body.text: By the window, with the round table.

body.speaker: Papa
body.text: Open the door. The first customer is here at seven.

body.speaker: Marco
body.text: It is six fifty now. Can I make the coffee?

body.speaker: Papa
body.text: Not yet. The machine is hot, and you are not tall enough.

body.speaker: Marco
body.text: But I am ten! I am tall enough for cups.

body.speaker: Papa
body.text: Good. Wash twenty cups and put them on the tray.

body.speaker: Marco
body.text: Done. Now can I help with the bread?

body.speaker: Papa
body.text: Yes. Cut six slices. Not too thin. We use them for toast.

body.speaker: Marco
body.text: Look, Papa! A customer is at the door already!

body.speaker: Papa
body.text: That is Signora Bianchi. She comes every Saturday at six fifty. Open the door and smile.

body.speaker: Marco
body.text: Good morning, Signora Bianchi! Coffee with milk and one biscuit?

body.speaker: Papa
body.text: Well done, Marco. You are a real café boy now.

annotations.exampleEn: We sat at the round table near the door.
The first customer arrives at seven.
He is not tall enough for that ride.
Put the cups on the tray.
He had toast and tea for breakfast.
Signora Bianchi is our oldest customer.
She had tea and one biscuit.
Well done, you finished the test!

grammarNotes.title: 'Enough' with adjectives
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Enough' đứng SAU tính từ để chỉ mức đủ: 'You are not tall enough.' / 'I am tall enough for cups.' Khác với 'too' đứng trước: 'too thin', 'too small'. Hai cấu trúc này luôn đi cùng nhau khi học về mức độ.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Enough' goes AFTER an adjective: 'You are not tall enough.' / 'I am tall enough for cups.' Compare with 'too' before the adjective: 'too thin', 'too small'. These two patterns are usually learned together.

grammarNotes.title: Imperatives for orders at work
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Người lớn hướng dẫn ngắn gọn bằng câu mệnh lệnh: 'Put the chairs on the floor.' / 'Wash twenty cups.' / 'Cut six slices.' / 'Open the door and smile.' Thêm 'please' để nghe lịch sự hơn.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Short instructions at work use imperatives: 'Put the chairs on the floor.' / 'Wash twenty cups.' / 'Cut six slices.' / 'Open the door and smile.' Add 'please' to soften.

translationVi: Marco: Bố ơi, đầu tiên con làm gì ạ? Bố: Chào con Marco. Con xếp ghế xuống sàn giúp bố nhé. Cô dọn vệ sinh để chúng trên bàn rồi. Marco: Dạ. Cái ghế nhỏ này để đâu hả bố? Bố: Để cạnh cửa sổ, đi với cái bàn tròn. Marco: Xong rồi! Tiếp theo là gì ạ? Bố: Mở cửa đi. Bảy giờ là khách đầu tiên đến rồi đấy. Marco: Bây giờ là sáu giờ năm mươi. Con pha cà phê được không bố? Bố: Chưa được đâu. Máy nóng lắm, mà con cũng chưa đủ cao. Marco: Nhưng con mười tuổi rồi! Đủ cao để xếp tách rồi. Bố: Tốt. Con rửa hai mươi cái tách rồi đặt lên khay. Marco: Xong rồi. Giờ con phụ bố làm bánh mì được không? Bố: Được. Cắt sáu lát. Đừng cắt mỏng quá. Mình dùng để nướng đó. Marco: Bố ơi nhìn kìa! Có khách đến cửa rồi! Bố: Đó là bà Bianchi. Thứ Bảy nào bà cũng đến đúng sáu giờ năm mươi. Mở cửa và cười với bà nhé. Marco: Chào bà Bianchi! Cà phê sữa và một cái bánh quy ạ? Bố: Giỏi lắm Marco. Giờ con đúng là cậu nhỏ của quán cà phê rồi.

questions.prompt: What does Papa ask Marco to do first?
options: Put the chairs on the floor
questions.explanation: 'Put the chairs on the floor, please.'

questions.prompt: Where does the small chair go?
options: By the window with the round table
questions.explanation: 'By the window, with the round table.'
questions.hint: Find the place for the small chair.

questions.prompt: When does the first customer arrive?
questions.explanation: 'The first customer is here at seven.'

options: The machine is hot and he is not tall enough
questions.explanation: 'The machine is hot, and you are not tall enough.'

questions.prompt: How many cups does Marco wash?
How many slices of bread does Marco cut?
Why does Papa say 'Not too thin'?
options: They use the bread for toast and need it thicker
Customers do not like thin bread

questions.explanation: 'She comes every Saturday at six fifty.'

options: Coffee with milk and one biscuit

questions.prompt: What does Papa say to praise Marco?
options: You are a real café boy now
questions.explanation: 'You are a real café boy now.'

cloze.template:
Marco: Papa, what can I do first?
Papa: Good morning, Marco. Put the {{b1}} on the floor, please. The cleaner left them on the tables.
Marco: Okay. Where does this small chair go?
Papa: By the {{b2}}, with the round table.
Marco: Done! What is next?
Papa: Open the door. The first {{b3}} is here at seven.
Marco: It is six fifty now. Can I make the {{b4}}?
Papa: Not yet. The machine is hot, and you are not {{b5}} enough.
Marco: But I am {{b6}}! I am tall enough for cups.
Papa: Good. Wash {{b7}} cups and put them on the tray.
Marco: Done. Now can I help with the bread?
Papa: Yes. Cut {{b8}} slices. Not too thin. We use them for {{b9}}.
Marco: Look, Papa! A customer is at the door already!
Papa: That is Signora Bianchi. She comes every {{b10}} at six fifty. Open the door and smile.
Marco: Good morning, Signora Bianchi! Coffee with milk and one {{b11}}?
Papa: Well done, Marco. You are a real café {{b12}} now.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Marco helps in the family café. What can children learn from helping at family work? What is good and what is hard about it?
